summary: Iteration 18: Complete testing of POST /api/operator/health-rules/{rule_name}/revert/{created_at} endpoint. All 10 test cases passed covering RBAC (401/403), validation (404/422), response format, full round-trip flow, cache invalidation, and audit entry structure.

rbac_tests.no_auth_401: PASS - POST revert with no auth returns 401
rbac_tests.guardian_403: PASS - POST revert with guardian token returns 403

validation_tests.unknown_rule_404: PASS - POST revert with unknown rule_name returns 404
validation_tests.invalid_timestamp_422: PASS - POST revert with invalid timestamp returns 422
validation_tests.nonexistent_audit_404: PASS - POST revert with non-existent audit entry returns 404

response_format_tests.no_internal_ids: PASS - No internal IDs (id, _id, changed_by) exposed in response

functionality_tests.full_round_trip: PASS - PUT update → GET audit timestamp → POST revert → GET verify original state restored
functionality_tests.cache_invalidation: PASS - GET immediately reflects reverted state (cache invalidated)
functionality_tests.audit_entry_structure: PASS - Revert creates audit entry with change_type='revert', old_config=state before revert, new_config=target's old_config

cleanup.restore_original_config: PASS - low_battery restored to: severity=low, cooldown=60, enabled=true, threshold_json={battery_percent:20, sustain_minutes:10, recovery_buffer:5}

critical_code_review_comments: Revert endpoint correctly uses {created_at:path} to handle + in timezone offsets
Atomic transaction: rule update + audit entry creation in same commit
Cache invalidation via rule_config_cache.invalidate() after commit
Response format correctly excludes internal IDs
context_for_next_testing_agent: Revert endpoint fully tested and working. Tests cover: (1) RBAC - 401 no auth, 403 guardian, (2) Validation - 404 unknown rule, 422 invalid timestamp, 404 nonexistent audit, (3) Full round-trip - update to high severity, revert to original low severity, verify via GET, (4) Audit entry - revert creates entry with change_type='revert', old_config=before state, new_config=reverted state. low_battery rule restored to original config after tests: severity=low, cooldown_minutes=60, enabled=true, threshold_json={battery_percent:20, sustain_minutes:10, recovery_buffer:5}.
